description: PR-11b visual-dashboard render review (companion to pr11b-visual-dashboard.md).
method: operator self-audit of cc_spine/dashboard_view.py plus a test suite, THEN an INDEPENDENT agent code review dispatched and COMPLETED before commit per governance §16.0. The reviewer walked every interpolation site for escaping, adversarially injected HTML-payload strings into every data field, walked the full reachable state space of the real readiness_report, and cross-checked the domain-gauge color logic against the actual hard-blocker gate.
independent_verdict: needs_work (1 major + 3 minor/nit; all fixed before commit)

independent_findings.problem: the domain gauge used a three-tier good(>=0.75)/warn(>=0.60)/bad(<0.60) classification, but the REAL hard-blocker gate in learner_state.readiness_report fires on accuracy < 0.75 with no warn tier. Verified end-to-end: a domain at 0.70 accuracy is an active hard blocker (readiness_report lists it, Attention card shows it red) yet the same domain's gauge rendered gauge-warn (amber) — the whole 0.60-0.749 band silently understated an active blocker's severity.
independent_findings.resolution: collapsed to a strict two-tone classification (good >=0.75 else bad), matching the gate exactly. The dead .gauge-warn CSS rule was removed so a future edit can't reach for it. Regression test sweeps the entire former warn band (0.0, 0.40, 0.60, 0.70, 0.749) asserting every value renders gauge-bad and never gauge-warn; a companion test asserts the CSS itself defines no .gauge-warn rule.

independent_findings.problem: the test overrode HTMLParser.error() to raise, but the stdlib html.parser has no strict/validating mode and never calls error() internally — the override is dead code, making the test close to tautological (it passes for almost any string).
independent_findings.resolution: replaced with a real structural check: a start/end-tag balance tracker (handle_starttag/handle_endtag) that asserts every non-void tag opened is closed in the right order.

independent_findings.problem: no test exercised a non-empty domain_accuracy or bank_accuracy dict, or asserted anything about the gauge-good/warn/bad classes — precisely the coverage gap that let F1 ship undetected.
independent_findings.resolution: added DomainGaugeTest (every value in the former warn band renders gauge-bad; the good boundary renders gauge-good; no .gauge-warn CSS rule exists; empty-state message) and BankGaugeTest (bank bars use gauge-accent, never a semantic color; empty-state message).

independent_findings.problem: the gauge fill-width clamp (min(100.0, ...)) bounded the upper end but not the lower, so a hypothetical negative accuracy would emit an invalid negative CSS width.
independent_findings.resolution: symmetric clamp max(0.0, min(100.0, ...)) applied in both cards. Not reachable via the current data model; defensive.

independent_findings.problem: the incomplete-state row only rendered `if not r['complete'] and r.get('note')` — an unstated dependency on the data model always supplying a truthy note whenever complete is False. A future data-model change violating that could silently produce an empty attention card for a genuinely not-ready state.
independent_findings.resolution: the incomplete branch now always renders a row when complete is False, falling back to a generic 'readiness is incomplete — see the readiness report for details' message if note is empty, removing the dependency on an unstated contract.

test_suite_bugs_found_and_fixed_during_r: test_ready_shows_good_chip_and_no_attention_card originally asserted assertNotIn('attn-card', html), which the CSS rule .attn-card{...} always satisfies-as-false regardless of whether the element renders — fixed (prior to this review, same session) to check the literal class="card attn-card" attribute.

The reviewer verified two NEW instances of the identical false-positive pattern surfaced while writing the F1/F3 regression tests (assertIn/assertNotIn on bare 'gauge-bad'/'gauge-accent', which the CSS stylesheet always contains) and these were corrected in the same pass to check the rendered class="gauge-fill gauge-X" attribute instead.
clean_areas_confirmed_by_reviewer: every data-derived interpolation site routes through html.escape — adversarially tested with injection payloads in hard_blockers, note, domain/bank keys, item ids, misconception ids, and the content-scale caveat; none executed or appeared unescaped
field-schema alignment: every dict access matches the exact PR-11a schema (domain uses 'attempts', bank uses 'total', mock/spaced_repetition/wrong_answers/top_misconceptions all line up)
determinism: no clock/random/locale calls; the view doesn't reorder the already-sorted PR-11a lists
the attention card produces at least one row for every reachable not-ready state (incomplete; complete+blockers; complete+no-blockers+below-target) — confirmed by walking the full state space
the '+N more due' honesty claim holds: no fabricated remainder exists for misconception lists, which have no comparable total in the data model
cmd_dashboard's --out crash on a nonexistent directory is consistent with existing CLI precedent (cmd_ingest has the identical behavior), not a new gap
Makefile dashboard-html-check and the CI workflow step are correctly wired into their chains
note: Independent review completed BEFORE this commit (the PR-9 lesson) and returned needs_work with one MAJOR finding — a miscalibrated gauge that could visually understate an active hard blocker. Caught pre-commit, not shipped. All five findings fixed; the report's design-plan color language and __init__.py docstring were also corrected to describe the strict two-tone calibration accurately.
